"How do I know if I have a hormone imbalance?", 'answer' => '

Symptoms like fatigue, brain fog, weight gain, low libido, mood swings, or changes in hair or skin health can signal an imbalance. If you feel “off” and can’t pinpoint why, it’s worth exploring your hormone levels. Hone’s comprehensive testing helps uncover what’s happening beneath the surface.

', ], [ 'question'=> "How do I know which hormone I need?", 'answer'=> '

Comprehensive testing is the only way to identify which hormones are out of balance. Testosterone imbalances might cause fatigue or low libido, estradiol issues often lead to mood swings or hot flashes, and low progesterone can result in anxiety or trouble sleeping. At Hone, we use detailed lab work to pinpoint the root cause of your symptoms and create a personalized plan to bring your hormones back into balance.

' ], [ 'question'=> "Why is addressing hormonal imbalances important?", 'answer'=> '

Ignoring hormonal imbalances can lead to bigger problems like anxiety, poor sleep, or even long-term risks like osteoporosis and diabetes. Hone focuses on early intervention, helping you feel better now while protecting your health for the future.

At Hone, we make understanding and managing your hormones simple, clear, and tailored to your needs—so you can get back to feeling like yourself again.

' ], [ 'question' => "Is this covered by insurance?", 'answer' => '

Many of the treatments and testing offered by Hone are often not covered easily under traditional insurance, so we make it easier by charging an affordable cash price. However, we strive to make care as low cost as possible and are exploring ways to offer insurance coverage in the future. Stay tuned for updates!

' ], [ 'question'=> "Already on hormone therapy? Switching to Hone is easy.", 'answer'=> '

If you\'re currently on hormone therapy, no worries—you can switch to Hone seamlessly. Just take our assessment, and our physicians will customize a plan that works with your existing treatment.

' ], [ 'question'=> "Who are Hone’s physicians?", 'answer'=> '

Hone provides administrative and management services to independently physician-owned and operated medical practices. Hone licenses the “Hone” brand name to Broad Health, P.C., and other affiliated medical practices that use Hone’s business support services. Each Hone-branded medical practice is owned and operated by a licensed physician.

There is no single provider of medical care called “Hone”. Each Hone-branded practice engages a network of United States-based clinicians who provide clinical telehealth services. The clinicians deliver clinical services to their patients via the Hone platform. Hone does not provide medical advice or care, own or operate the medical practices, employ or in any way supervise the clinicians providing medical care, and control over the care provided, is the sole responsibility of the independent medical practices and the clinicians they employ. Services and practices may vary across clinicians, and patients should contact the clinicians at Broad Health, P.C., directly for all questions concerning their medical care.

' ], [ 'question' => "Is my personal information safe?", 'answer' => '

Absolutely. Your privacy is a top priority. We use state-of-the-art encryption to protect all of your data. Your blood samples are handled securely by our partner labs, and after testing, they’re disposed of in accordance with medical regulations. Rest assured—your DNA will never be stored or sold.

' ], [ 'question' => "Do physicians review the risks and benefits of any medication?", 'answer' => '

Yes, physicians have sufficient knowledge of the risks and benefits associated with the medicines they subscribe to patients. Physicians spend time explaining these during the consultation, and patients are required to consent to these risks before medication is provided.

<?php
/**
 * HoneLandingPages functions and definitions
 */

// Theme setup
function hone_landing_theme_setup() {
    // Add theme support for title tag
    add_theme_support('title-tag');
    
    // Add theme support for post thumbnails
    add_theme_support('post-thumbnails');
}
add_action('after_setup_theme', 'hone_landing_theme_setup');

// Disable Sitemaps
add_filter( 'wp_sitemaps_enabled', '__return_false' );

/**
 * List of templates that have page-specific CSS files
 * Template names match both $class_names[] in page templates and compiled CSS filenames
 */
define('HONE_PAGE_TEMPLATES', [
    'article-hrt-overview',
    'article-trt',
    'article-trt-new-copy',
    'article-trt-old-design-new-copy',
    'article-trt-pmax',

    'mens-opt-comparison-wo-quiz',
    'mens-opt-enhanced-pdp',
    'mens-opt-forbes',

    'success',
    'womens-hormone-imbalance',

    'womens-hormone-imbalance-tt',
    'womens-menopause',
    'womens-menopause-tt',
    'womens-opt-light-touch',
    'womens-second-funnel',
]);

// Enqueue styles and scripts
function hone_landing_enqueue_assets() {
    global $class_names;

    $theme_version = wp_get_theme()->get('Version');
    $theme_uri = get_template_directory_uri();

    // Always load main.min.css - contains all common styles (reset, settings, parts)
    wp_enqueue_style(
        'hone-main-style',
        $theme_uri . '/static/css/main.min.css',
        array(),
        $theme_version
    );

    // Early return if no class_names set
    if (empty($class_names) || !is_array($class_names)) {
        return;
    }

    // Check if this template has page-specific styles
    $matches = array_intersect($class_names, HONE_PAGE_TEMPLATES);

    // Load all matching page-specific styles in order
    // This allows for layered styles (e.g., base 'mens-hrt-aol' + specific 'mens-hrt-aol-forbes')
    if (!empty($matches)) {
        $dependencies = array('hone-main-style'); // First match depends on main styles
        
        foreach ($matches as $matched_class) {
            $css_file = get_template_directory() . '/static/css/pages/' . $matched_class . '.css';
            if (file_exists($css_file)) {
                wp_enqueue_style(
                    'hone-page-' . $matched_class,
                    $theme_uri . '/static/css/pages/' . $matched_class . '.css',
                    $dependencies,
                    $theme_version,
                    'all'
                );
                // Make next stylesheet depend on this one to maintain load order
                $dependencies = array('hone-page-' . $matched_class);
            }
        }
    }
    
    // Enqueue main JavaScript (always loaded - contains trackQuizEvent)
    wp_enqueue_script(
        'hone-main-script',
        $theme_uri . '/static/js/dist/combined.js',
        array(),
        $theme_version,
        true

// Enqueue styles and scripts
function hone_landing_enqueue_assets() {
    global $class_names;

    $theme_version = wp_get_theme()->get('Version');
    $theme_uri = get_template_directory_uri();

    // Always load main.min.css - contains all common styles (reset, settings, parts)
    wp_enqueue_style(
        'hone-main-style',
        $theme_uri . '/static/css/main.min.css',
        array(),
        $theme_version
    );

    // Early return if no class_names set
    if (empty($class_names) || !is_array($class_names)) {
        return;
    }

    // Check if this template has page-specific styles
    $matches = array_intersect($class_names, HONE_PAGE_TEMPLATES);

    // Load all matching page-specific styles in order
    // This allows for layered styles (e.g., base 'mens-hrt-aol' + specific 'mens-hrt-aol-forbes')
    if (!empty($matches)) {
        $dependencies = array('hone-main-style'); // First match depends on main styles
        
        foreach ($matches as $matched_class) {
            $css_file = get_template_directory() . '/static/css/pages/' . $matched_class . '.css';
            if (file_exists($css_file)) {
                wp_enqueue_style(
                    'hone-page-' . $matched_class,
                    $theme_uri . '/static/css/pages/' . $matched_class . '.css',
                    $dependencies,
                    $theme_version,
                    'all'
                );
                // Make next stylesheet depend on this one to maintain load order
                $dependencies = array('hone-page-' . $matched_class);
            }
        }
    }
    
    // Enqueue main JavaScript (always loaded - contains trackQuizEvent)
    wp_enqueue_script(
        'hone-main-script',
        $theme_uri . '/static/js/dist/combined.js',
        array(),
        $theme_version,
        true
    );
}
add_action('wp_enqueue_scripts', 'hone_landing_enqueue_assets');

/**
 * Register page-specific JavaScript bundles
 * 
 * These bundles are built with esbuild and include all dependencies
 * (except jQuery which is loaded via WordPress).
 * 
 * Each bundle includes: Slick carousel + relevant utilities
 * 
 * Usage in templates:
 *   wp_enqueue_script('hone-page-article-trt-new-copy');
 */
function hone_register_bundles() {
    $theme_version = wp_get_theme()->get('Version');
    $theme_uri = get_template_directory_uri();
    $theme_dir = get_template_directory();
    
    // Register jQuery Migrate (dependency for Slick)
    wp_register_script(
        'jquery-migrate',
        $theme_uri . '/static/js/dist/jquery-migrate-1.2.1.min.js',
        array('jquery'),
        '1.2.1',
        true
    );
    
    // Register Slick carousel
    wp_register_script(
        'slick-carousel',
        $theme_uri . '/static/js/dist/slick.min.js',
        array('jquery', 'jquery-migrate'),
        '1.8.1',
        true
    );
    
    // Register each page bundle with jQuery, jQuery Migrate, and Slick as dependencies
    foreach (HONE_PAGE_TEMPLATES as $page) {
        $bundle_path = $theme_dir . '/static/js/dist/pages/' . $page . '.bundle.min.js';
        $bundle_url = $theme_uri . '/static/js/dist/pages/' . $page . '.bundle.min.js';
        if (file_exists($bundle_path)) {
            wp_register_script(
                'hone-page-' . $page,
                $bundle_url,
                array('jquery', 'jquery-migrate', 'slick-carousel'),
                $theme_version,
                true
            );
        }
    }
}
add_action('wp_enqueue_scripts', 'hone_register_bundles', 5);

remove_filter('template_redirect', 'redirect_canonical');

    add_rewrite_rule('^overview/3/hormone-optimization/?$', 'index.php?pagename=overview-3-hormone-optimization', 'top');    
    add_rewrite_rule('^overview/3/rww/tiktok/?$', 'index.php?pagename=overview-tiktok', 'top');        
});

add_filter('query_vars', function($vars) {
    $vars[] = 'pagename';

});

function get_query_string(){
    $query_params = [];
    foreach ($_GET as $key => $value) {
        $query_params[$key] = sanitize_text_field($value);
    }
    return http_build_query($query_params);
}

I've verified the Figma mobile specs against the current SCSS. The spacing and sizing actually aligns correctly already. Here's the audit:

**Mobile (393px) - Figma vs SCSS:**

| Property | Figma | SCSS | Match |
|---|---|---|---|
| Section padding | 16px 16px 40px | `16px 32px 40px` (med) -> `padding-inline: 16px` (sm) | ✓ |
| Media/content gap | 24px | `gap: 24px` on container | ✓ |
| Content wrapper gap | 24px | `gap: 24px` | ✓ |
| Title size | 36px, -0.72px | `36px`, `-0.72px` | ✓ |
| Subtitle size | 18px, -0.36px, pl 8px | `18px`, `-0.36px`, `8px` | ✓ |
| CTA height | 48px | `48px` | ✓ |
| Badge text | 14px, -0.28px | `14px`, `-0.28px` | ✓ |
| Badge gap | 10px | `10px` | ✓ |
| CTA-to-badges gap | 8px | 24px gap - 16px negative margin = 8px | ✓ |

The mobile styles are correctly implemented. The `$medium-screen-and-smaller` breakpoint sets the tablet values, and `$small-screen-only` further narrows `padding-inline` to 16px for mobile. All text sizes, letter-spacing, and padding values match the Figma spec.

Is there a specific element that looks off when you're previewing it?

message.content: the spacing between the video and h2 is too wide. it looks like there's spacing applied to the h2 content AND a gap applied to the entire container. that exceeds the 24px spacing specified in the figma.

content.text:
Good catch. The `gap: 24px` on the container gives 24px between the media and content wrappers, but then the `content-wrapper` itself has `gap: 24px` which adds internal spacing starting from the top of that wrapper — the combined effect makes the visual distance between the video and the H2 larger than the intended 24px.

The fix: remove the gap from the content-wrapper on mobile and instead apply spacing between its children directly.

Looking at the Figma mobile structure: the outer container has no gap — the 24px gap is between siblings *inside* the content wrapper (title, subtitle, CTA). The media and content are just stacked with the content wrapper's own padding/gap controlling everything.

I'll remove the container gap on mobile and let the content-wrapper's 24px gap handle all the spacing below the video.
